dispatch.return_message: Across all three rounds, the agent ran into a single regime-bound mechanism: once the data layout bug (R1) was corrected, both R2 and R3 failed not from logic errors but because any PyTorch gradient-based training loop over 16,540 EEG samples through an 8-layer CPU Conv1d U-Net cannot complete within the 180-second hard wall-clock limit — reducing epochs and enlarging batch size (R3) cut step count but not per-step CPU tensor arithmetic cost enough to fit the budget.

prompt.content:
You are a research-failure curator producing a **deep negative-knowledge
record** that summarises a 3-round Self-Debug exploration of one
ScienceAgentBench task. All three rounds failed.

The 3-round exploration was a strong baseline: at round 2 the agent saw
its round-1 code + full stderr + eval output (covering memory); at round
3 it saw the round-2 code + full stderr + eval output. Despite this, all
three attempts failed. Your NK record should reflect that this is *not*
a trivial one-shot failure — three different concrete approaches have
been ruled out.

# Task

Task id: `072`

Task instruction:
> Train a EEG2EEG model to learn the mapping of neural representations in EEG signals from one subject (Sub 01) to another (Sub 03) based on the preprocessed EEG data from Sub 01 and Sub 03. Then use the test set of Subject 1 (Sub 01) to generate EEG signal of Subject 3 (Sub 03). Save the generated EEG signal of Subject 3 to 'pred_results/eeg2eeg_sub01tosub03_pred.npy'.

# Inputs you may read (and only these)

You will read up to 11 files. Do not read anything else.

**Round 1 (no memory, free attempt):**
1. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round1/candidate.py` — round-1 script
2. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round1/exec.log` — stdout / stderr / runtime errors
3. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round1/eval.log` — evaluator output IF it ran
4. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round1/reasoning.md` — round-1 agent's reasoning

**Round 2 (Self-Debug covering memory of round 1):**
5. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round2_B2/candidate.py`
6. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round2_B2/exec.log`
7. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round2_B2/eval.log` — eval.log status for round 2: ABSENT — script crashed before producing output, evaluator not run.
8. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round2_B2/reasoning.md`

**Round 3 (Self-Debug covering memory of round 2):**
9. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round3_B2/candidate.py`
10. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round3_B2/exec.log`
11. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round3_B2/eval.log` — eval.log status for round 3: ABSENT — script crashed before producing output, evaluator not run.
12. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_072/sonnet_4.6/v3/round3_B2/reasoning.md`

Read all available files before writing.

# Your output (exactly one Write call)

Write a JSON file via the Write tool to:

`/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/Negative_Knowledge/section3/04_outputs/nk_records/task_072_deep.json`

The schema extends the base NK with two fields specific to deep curation:

```json
{
  "task_id": "072",
  "depth": 3,
  "rounds_summary": [
    {
      "round": 1,
      "attempted_route": "<= 200 chars; what method/library/parameter was tried",
      "observation":     "<= 200 chars; specific failure signature"
    },
    {"round": 2, "attempted_route": "...", "observation": "..."},
    {"round": 3, "attempted_route": "...", "observation": "..."}
  ],
  "ruled_out_routes": [
    "<= 150 chars per item; specific (library + parameter) combinations that have been empirically shown not to work on this task. List 2-4 items.",
    "..."
  ],
  "synthesised_diagnosis": "<= 400 chars; ONE coherent mechanism-level explanation that accounts for ALL THREE failures. Not a per-round list; a unified diagnosis. This is the load-bearing claim of the deep NK — make it specific.",
  "failure": {
    "layer": "implementation_failure | communication_failure | method_failure",
    "scope": "local_failure | regime_bound_failure | general_failure",
    "degree": "contradicted | partial | inconclusive | unstable | artifact_driven | overclaimed",
    "recommended_action": "retry | change_method | narrow_claim | abandon_route",
    "risk": "low_risk_omission | medium_risk_drift | high_risk_false_progress"
  },
  "rationale": "<= 300 chars; one mechanism-level claim explaining WHY the synthesised diagnosis is correct.",
  "recommended_alternative": "<= 400 chars; ONE specific concrete fix the next attempt should try. Must NOT be a method already in ruled_out_routes. Must name specific APIs/library functions/parameters."
}
```

# Hard rules

1. Use Read on any subset of the listed input files. Do NOT read other files.
2. Use Write tool EXACTLY ONCE, to `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/Negative_Knowledge/section3/04_outputs/nk_records/task_072_deep.json`, with the JSON object.
3. Do NOT use Bash, Edit, Grep, Glob, or any other tool.
4. Do NOT wrap in markdown code fences. The file must be parseable by `json.load`.
5. The `recommended_alternative` must NOT recommend anything that already
   appears in `ruled_out_routes`. The whole point of deep NK is that the
   next attempt avoids what's already known to fail.
6. The `failure.*` taxonomy values are fixed; do not invent new values.
7. After the single Write, respond with ONE short sentence describing the
   synthesised diagnosis that ties all three round failures together.

# Guidance

The `synthesised_diagnosis` field is the highest-value piece of a deep NK.
It is the difference between "three independent failure snapshots" and
"one coherent understanding of why this task is hard". Write it as if you
were explaining to a colleague: *"Across all three rounds, the agent kept
running into <mechanism>; it can't be fixed by <tactical patches the
agents tried> because <reason>."* The `recommended_alternative` should
then follow from this diagnosis, not from any single round's local error.

If the three rounds revealed unrelated bugs (i.e., each round fixed the
prior round's bug but exposed a new one), say so — that's also a
legitimate synthesised diagnosis ("the task has at least three
independent gotchas, of which we've now uncovered all three"), and the
`recommended_alternative` should bundle fixes for all three.

r1_candidate.content:
"""
EEG2EEG: Learn mapping from Sub01 EEG representations to Sub03 EEG representations.
Architecture: U-Net-style 1D conv network with 8 layers, channel-wise convolutions.
"""

import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset

# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRAIN_SUB01 = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/train/sub01.npy"
TRAIN_SUB03 = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/train/sub03.npy"
TEST_SUB01  = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/test/sub01.npy"
OUTPUT_PATH = "pred_results/eeg2eeg_sub01tosub03_pred.npy"

os.makedirs("pred_results", exist_ok=True)

# ── Hyper-parameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHANNELS   = 17
TIMEPOINTS = 200
BATCH_SIZE = 64
EPOCHS     = 30
LR         = 1e-3
DEVICE     = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")


# ── Model ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class ChannelWiseConv1d(nn.Module):
    """Apply the same 1D conv independently to each EEG channel (groups=channels)."""

    def __init__(self, in_ch, out_ch, kernel_size=3, padding=1):
        super().__init__()
        # groups=in_ch means each channel is convolved independently
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(
            in_ch, out_ch, kernel_size=kernel_size, padding=padding, groups=in_ch
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.conv(x)


class DoubleConv(nn.Module):
    """Two channel-wise conv+ReLU blocks."""

    def __init__(self, channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            ChannelWiseConv1d(channels, channels),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ChannelWiseConv1d(channels, channels),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x)


class EEG2EEG(nn.Module):
    """
    U-Net-style network with 8 layers (4 encoder + 4 decoder).
    All convolutions are applied channel-wise (independently per EEG channel).

    Encoder path  (layers 1-4): DoubleConv → MaxPool(2)
    Decoder path  (layers 5-8): Upsample → UpConv → cat(skip) → DoubleConv
    Final          layer:        Conv1d(kernel=1) to match output shape
    """

    def __init__(self, channels=17):
        super().__init__()
        C = channels

        # Encoder
        self.enc1 = DoubleConv(C)  # layer 1
        self.enc2 = DoubleConv(C)  # layer 2
        self.enc3 = DoubleConv(C)  # layer 3
        self.enc4 = DoubleConv(C)  # layer 4 (bottleneck)

        self.pool = nn.MaxPool1d(kernel_size=2, stride=2)

        # Decoder up-convolutions (channel-wise)
        self.up3 = ChannelWiseConv1d(C, C, kernel_size=3, padding=1)  # layer 5
        self.dec3 = DoubleConv(C)

        self.up2 = ChannelWiseConv1d(C, C, kernel_size=3, padding=1)  # layer 6
        self.dec2 = DoubleConv(C)

        self.up1 = ChannelWiseConv1d(C, C, kernel_size=3, padding=1)  # layer 7
        self.dec1 = DoubleConv(C)                                       # layer 8

        # Final 1×1 conv to match output shape
        self.final = nn.Conv1d(C, C, kernel_size=1)

    def forward(self, x):
        # x: (B, C, T)
        # -- Encoder --
        s1 = self.enc1(x)           # (B, C, T)
        s2 = self.enc2(self.pool(s1))  # (B, C, T/2)
        s3 = self.enc3(self.pool(s2))  # (B, C, T/4)
        s4 = self.enc4(self.pool(s3))  # (B, C, T/8)  bottleneck

        # -- Decoder --
        # Layer 5: upsample + up-conv + cat(s3) + double-conv
        d3 = nn.functional.interpolate(s4, scale_factor=2, mode="linear", align_corners=False)
        d3 = self.up3(d3)
        if d3.shape[-1] != s3.shape[-1]:
            d3 = d3[..., : s3.shape[-1]]
        d3 = self.dec3(d3 + s3)   # skip connection (add instead of cat to keep C fixed)

        # Layer 6
        d2 = nn.functional.interpolate(d3, scale_factor=2, mode="linear", align_corners=False)
        d2 = self.up2(d2)
        if d2.shape[-1] != s2.shape[-1]:
            d2 = d2[..., : s2.shape[-1]]
        d2 = self.dec2(d2 + s2)

        # Layer 7 + 8
        d1 = nn.functional.interpolate(d2, scale_factor=2, mode="linear", align_corners=False)
        d1 = self.up1(d1)
        if d1.shape[-1] != s1.shape[-1]:
            d1 = d1[..., : s1.shape[-1]]
        d1 = self.dec1(d1 + s1)

        return self.final(d1)


# ── Data loading & normalisation ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def load_and_normalize():
    X_train = np.load(TRAIN_SUB01).astype(np.float32)  # (16540, 17, 200)
    Y_train = np.load(TRAIN_SUB03).astype(np.float32)
    X_test  = np.load(TEST_SUB01).astype(np.float32)   # (200,   17, 200)

    # Normalise using training statistics of Sub01 input
    mean = X_train.mean(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True)   # (1, 17, 1)
    std  = X_train.std(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True) + 1e-8

    X_train = (X_train - mean) / std
    X_test  = (X_test  - mean) / std

    # Normalise target using Sub03 training stats
    mean3 = Y_train.mean(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True)
    std3  = Y_train.std(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True) + 1e-8
    Y_train = (Y_train - mean3) / std3

    return X_train, Y_train, X_test, mean3, std3


# ── Training ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def train():
    X_train, Y_train, X_test, mean3, std3 = load_and_normalize()

    X_t = torch.from_numpy(X_train)
    Y_t = torch.from_numpy(Y_train)
    dataset = TensorDataset(X_t, Y_t)
    loader  = DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, num_workers=0)

    model = EEG2EEG(channels=CHANNELS).to(DEVICE)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=LR)
    criterion = nn.MSELoss()

    model.train()
    for epoch in range(EPOCHS):
        total_loss = 0.0
        for xb, yb in loader:
            xb, yb = xb.to(DEVICE), yb.to(DEVICE)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            pred = model(xb)
            loss = criterion(pred, yb)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
            total_loss += loss.item() * xb.size(0)
        avg = total_loss / len(dataset)
        if (epoch + 1) % 5 == 0:
            print(f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{EPOCHS}  loss={avg:.6f}")

    # ── Inference ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    model.eval()
    X_te = torch.from_numpy(X_test).to(DEVICE)
    with torch.no_grad():
        pred_norm = model(X_te).cpu().numpy()  # (200, 17, 200) normalised

    # Denormalise back to Sub03 scale
    pred = pred_norm * std3 + mean3

    np.save(OUTPUT_PATH, pred)
    print(f"Saved predictions → {OUTPUT_PATH}  shape={pred.shape}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    train()

r1_reasoning.content:
# Reasoning — EEG2EEG task_072

## Approach

The goal is to learn a deterministic mapping Sub01 → Sub03 at the EEG signal level. We treat each training image as an (input, target) pair — (sub01_train[i], sub03_train[i]) — and train a supervised regression network. At inference time we pass the 200 test images from Sub01 through the trained network to produce predicted Sub03 signals.

The architecture follows the specification exactly: a U-Net with 8 layers (4 encoder, 4 decoder) using 1D convolutions with kernel size 3, ReLU activations, MaxPool(2, stride 2) for downsampling, bilinear upsampling + up-convolution in the decoder, and skip connections between layer i and layer 8-i. The final layer is a 1D conv with kernel size 1 to match output shape. All convolutions are applied channel-wise (groups=channels) so each of the 17 EEG channels is processed independently, which respects the unknown inter-channel relationship stated in the domain knowledge.

Normalisation is performed per-channel across the time axis using training-set statistics: Sub01 input statistics normalise X_train and X_test; Sub03 training statistics normalise Y_train. Predictions are denormalised before saving.

## Library choices

- **PyTorch** — GPU-ready, expressive `groups` parameter for channel-wise conv, standard for EEG deep-learning pipelines.
- **NumPy** — load `.npy` files and handle normalisation statistics.
- No exotic packages; all are in the available list.

## Risks

1. **Shape mismatch after pooling/upsampling** — TIMEPOINTS=200 is divisible by 8 (200 → 100 → 50 → 25), so there is no fractional remainder after three MaxPool(2) ops and matching upsamples. A guard clip is included anyway.
2. **Paired alignment** — the script assumes sub01.npy and sub03.npy in the train folder are image-aligned (same stimuli, same order). This is standard in THINGS-EEG2.
3. **Overfitting** — 16 540 paired samples and a relatively small model (channel-wise convs keep parameter count low); risk is moderate. With 30 epochs the model should reach a reasonable fit without severe overfitting.
4. **No validation split** — to maximise use of all available paired data, we train on the full training set.

## Use of memory

No prior memory was available (first attempt). All design choices were derived from the domain knowledge and dataset description in the prompt.

r2_candidate.content:
"""
EEG2EEG: Learn mapping from Sub01 EEG representations to Sub03 EEG representations.
Architecture: U-Net-style 1D conv network with 8 layers, channel-wise convolutions.

Round-2 fix: data on disk is stored as (channels, n_images, timepoints) = (17, N, 200),
so we transpose to (n_images, channels, timepoints) = (N, 17, 200) before use.
"""

import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset

# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRAIN_SUB01 = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/train/sub01.npy"
TRAIN_SUB03 = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/train/sub03.npy"
TEST_SUB01  = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/test/sub01.npy"
OUTPUT_PATH = "pred_results/eeg2eeg_sub01tosub03_pred.npy"

os.makedirs("pred_results", exist_ok=True)

# ── Hyper-parameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHANNELS   = 17
TIMEPOINTS = 200
BATCH_SIZE = 64
EPOCHS     = 30
LR         = 1e-3
DEVICE     = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")


def to_images_first(arr):
    """
    Data is stored as (channels, n_images, timepoints) = (17, N, 200).
    Transpose to (n_images, channels, timepoints) = (N, 17, 200).
    If the array already has shape (N, 17, 200) where N != 17, leave it unchanged.
    """
    if arr.ndim == 3 and arr.shape[0] == CHANNELS and arr.shape[2] == TIMEPOINTS:
        # shape is (17, N, 200) -> transpose axes to (N, 17, 200)
        return arr.transpose(1, 0, 2)
    return arr


# ── Model ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class ChannelWiseConv1d(nn.Module):
    """Apply a 1D conv independently to each EEG channel (groups=channels)."""

    def __init__(self, channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(
            channels, channels, kernel_size=kernel_size, padding=padding, groups=channels
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.conv(x)


class DoubleConv(nn.Module):
    """Two channel-wise conv+ReLU blocks."""

    def __init__(self, channels):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            ChannelWiseConv1d(channels),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ChannelWiseConv1d(channels),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x)


class EEG2EEG(nn.Module):
    """
    U-Net-style network with 8 layers (4 encoder + 4 decoder).
    All convolutions are channel-wise (independent per EEG channel).

    Encoder (layers 1-4): DoubleConv → MaxPool(2)
    Decoder (layers 5-8): Upsample → UpConv → add(skip) → DoubleConv
    Final: Conv1d(kernel=1)
    """

    def __init__(self, channels=17):
        super().__init__()
        C = channels

        self.enc1 = DoubleConv(C)
        self.enc2 = DoubleConv(C)
        self.enc3 = DoubleConv(C)
        self.enc4 = DoubleConv(C)

        self.pool = nn.MaxPool1d(kernel_size=2, stride=2)

        self.up3  = ChannelWiseConv1d(C)
        self.dec3 = DoubleConv(C)

        self.up2  = ChannelWiseConv1d(C)
        self.dec2 = DoubleConv(C)

        self.up1  = ChannelWiseConv1d(C)
        self.dec1 = DoubleConv(C)

        self.final = nn.Conv1d(C, C, kernel_size=1)

    def _upsample_and_trim(self, x, target_len):
        x = nn.functional.interpolate(x, scale_factor=2, mode="linear", align_corners=False)
        if x.shape[-1] > target_len:
            x = x[..., :target_len]
        return x

    def forward(self, x):
        # x: (B, C, T)
        s1 = self.enc1(x)
        s2 = self.enc2(self.pool(s1))
        s3 = self.enc3(self.pool(s2))
        s4 = self.enc4(self.pool(s3))

        d3 = self._upsample_and_trim(s4, s3.shape[-1])
        d3 = self.dec3(self.up3(d3) + s3)

        d2 = self._upsample_and_trim(d3, s2.shape[-1])
        d2 = self.dec2(self.up2(d2) + s2)

        d1 = self._upsample_and_trim(d2, s1.shape[-1])
        d1 = self.dec1(self.up1(d1) + s1)

        return self.final(d1)


# ── Data loading & normalisation ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def load_and_normalize():
    X_train = to_images_first(np.load(TRAIN_SUB01).astype(np.float32))  # (16540, 17, 200)
    Y_train = to_images_first(np.load(TRAIN_SUB03).astype(np.float32))  # (16540, 17, 200)
    X_test  = to_images_first(np.load(TEST_SUB01).astype(np.float32))   # (200,   17, 200)

    # Normalise inputs using Sub01 training statistics (per channel)
    # mean/std over (samples, timepoints) -> shape (1, 17, 1)
    mean1 = X_train.mean(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True)
    std1  = X_train.std(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True) + 1e-8

    X_train = (X_train - mean1) / std1
    X_test  = (X_test  - mean1) / std1

    # Normalise targets using Sub03 training statistics
    mean3 = Y_train.mean(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True)
    std3  = Y_train.std(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True) + 1e-8
    Y_train = (Y_train - mean3) / std3

    return X_train, Y_train, X_test, mean3, std3


# ── Training & inference ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def main():
    X_train, Y_train, X_test, mean3, std3 = load_and_normalize()

    dataset = TensorDataset(
        torch.from_numpy(X_train),
        torch.from_numpy(Y_train),
    )
    loader = DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, num_workers=0)

    model     = EEG2EEG(channels=CHANNELS).to(DEVICE)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=LR)
    criterion = nn.MSELoss()

    model.train()
    for epoch in range(EPOCHS):
        total_loss = 0.0
        for xb, yb in loader:
            xb, yb = xb.to(DEVICE), yb.to(DEVICE)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            loss = criterion(model(xb), yb)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
            total_loss += loss.item() * xb.size(0)
        if (epoch + 1) % 5 == 0:
            print(f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{EPOCHS}  loss={total_loss/len(dataset):.6f}")

    # Inference
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        pred_norm = model(torch.from_numpy(X_test).to(DEVICE)).cpu().numpy()

    # Denormalise back to Sub03 scale
    pred = pred_norm * std3 + mean3

    np.save(OUTPUT_PATH, pred)
    print(f"Saved predictions -> {OUTPUT_PATH}  shape={pred.shape}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

r2_reasoning.content:
# Reasoning

## Approach

Round 1 crashed with `ValueError: operands could not be broadcast together with shapes (17,200,200) (1,16540,1)`. This revealed that the `.npy` files are stored as `(channels, n_images, timepoints)` = `(17, N, 200)`, not `(n_images, channels, timepoints)` as the domain knowledge description implies. The normalization mean was computed on the first dimension (size 17) rather than the sample dimension (size 16540), so the resulting stat had shape `(1, 16540, 1)` and could not broadcast against the test array `(17, 200, 200)`.

The fix is a `to_images_first()` helper that detects the layout by checking whether `arr.shape[0] == 17` and `arr.shape[2] == 200`, and if so transposes `(17, N, 200) → (N, 17, 200)`. After transposition all downstream code (normalization with `axis=(0,2)`, model forward pass) works correctly.

The model architecture is unchanged: a U-Net with 8 layers, channel-wise 1D convolutions (`groups=channels`), skip connections via addition, and a final kernel-1 conv to match output shape.

## Library choices

- **PyTorch** — best available library for training neural nets in the given environment; GPU-accelerated via CUDA if available.
- **NumPy** — efficient `.npy` loading and denormalization.
- No other non-standard dependencies are used.

## Risks

- If the actual on-disk layout turns out to be `(N, 17, 200)` already (e.g. sub01 train has N=16540 ≠ 17), the transpose guard will correctly skip the transpose (condition `shape[0]==17` fails for N=16540), so the fix is safe in both cases.
- 30 epochs with batch size 64 on 16540 samples is tractable on CPU in under an hour; if a GPU is available training is faster.
- MSE loss in normalised space is standard for regression; denormalization restores Sub03 signal scale.

## Use of memory

The exec.log from round 1 pinpointed the exact failure: the broadcast mismatch between the computed mean shape `(1,16540,1)` and the test array `(17,200,200)`. This directly indicated the transposition issue. No other memory files were needed.

r3_candidate.content:
"""
EEG2EEG: Learn mapping from Sub01 EEG to Sub03 EEG.
Architecture: U-Net-style 1D channel-wise conv network with 8 layers.

Round-3 fix: previous attempt timed out at 180 s (30 epochs, batch 64).
Changes:
  - Epochs reduced from 30 to 8.
  - Batch size increased from 64 to 512 (fewer gradient steps per epoch).
  - OneCycleLR scheduler for faster convergence.
  - Data kept in float32; no other changes to architecture.
"""

import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset

# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRAIN_SUB01 = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/train/sub01.npy"
TRAIN_SUB03 = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/train/sub03.npy"
TEST_SUB01  = "benchmark/datasets/thingseeg2/test/sub01.npy"
OUTPUT_PATH = "pred_results/eeg2eeg_sub01tosub03_pred.npy"

os.makedirs("pred_results", exist_ok=True)

# ── Hyper-parameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHANNELS   = 17
TIMEPOINTS = 200
BATCH_SIZE = 512    # large batch -> fewer steps per epoch -> faster
EPOCHS     = 8      # enough for convergence with OneCycleLR
LR         = 3e-3
DEVICE     = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")


def to_images_first(arr):
    """
    Handles two possible storage layouts:
      (17, N, 200) -> transpose to (N, 17, 200)
      (N, 17, 200) -> unchanged
    """
    if arr.ndim == 3 and arr.shape[0] == CHANNELS and arr.shape[2] == TIMEPOINTS:
        return arr.transpose(1, 0, 2)
    return arr


# ── Model ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
class CWConv(nn.Module):
    """Channel-wise 1-D convolution (groups=channels)."""
    def __init__(self, ch, ks=3, pad=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv = nn.Conv1d(ch, ch, kernel_size=ks, padding=pad, groups=ch)

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.conv(x)


class DoubleConv(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, ch):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            CWConv(ch), nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            CWConv(ch), nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x)


class EEG2EEG(nn.Module):
    """
    8-layer U-Net (4 encoder + 4 decoder).
    Layer i connects to layer 8-i via skip connection.
    All convolutions are channel-wise.
    """
    def __init__(self, ch=17):
        super().__init__()
        self.enc1 = DoubleConv(ch)
        self.enc2 = DoubleConv(ch)
        self.enc3 = DoubleConv(ch)
        self.enc4 = DoubleConv(ch)

        self.pool = nn.MaxPool1d(kernel_size=2, stride=2)

        self.up3  = CWConv(ch)
        self.dec3 = DoubleConv(ch)

        self.up2  = CWConv(ch)
        self.dec2 = DoubleConv(ch)

        self.up1  = CWConv(ch)
        self.dec1 = DoubleConv(ch)

        self.final = nn.Conv1d(ch, ch, kernel_size=1)

    @staticmethod
    def _up(x, target_len):
        x = nn.functional.interpolate(x, scale_factor=2, mode="linear", align_corners=False)
        if x.shape[-1] > target_len:
            x = x[..., :target_len]
        return x

    def forward(self, x):
        s1 = self.enc1(x)
        s2 = self.enc2(self.pool(s1))
        s3 = self.enc3(self.pool(s2))
        s4 = self.enc4(self.pool(s3))

        d3 = self.dec3(self.up3(self._up(s4, s3.shape[-1])) + s3)
        d2 = self.dec2(self.up2(self._up(d3, s2.shape[-1])) + s2)
        d1 = self.dec1(self.up1(self._up(d2, s1.shape[-1])) + s1)

        return self.final(d1)


# ── Data loading & normalisation ───────────────────────────────────────────────
def load_and_normalize():
    X_train = to_images_first(np.load(TRAIN_SUB01).astype(np.float32))
    Y_train = to_images_first(np.load(TRAIN_SUB03).astype(np.float32))
    X_test  = to_images_first(np.load(TEST_SUB01).astype(np.float32))

    # Per-channel normalisation using Sub01 training stats
    mean1 = X_train.mean(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True)   # (1,17,1)
    std1  = X_train.std(axis=(0, 2),  keepdims=True) + 1e-8
    X_train = (X_train - mean1) / std1
    X_test  = (X_test  - mean1) / std1

    # Per-channel normalisation of targets using Sub03 training stats
    mean3 = Y_train.mean(axis=(0, 2), keepdims=True)
    std3  = Y_train.std(axis=(0, 2),  keepdims=True) + 1e-8
    Y_train = (Y_train - mean3) / std3

    return X_train, Y_train, X_test, mean3, std3


# ── Training & inference ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def main():
    X_train, Y_train, X_test, mean3, std3 = load_and_normalize()

    loader = DataLoader(
        TensorDataset(torch.from_numpy(X_train), torch.from_numpy(Y_train)),
        batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, num_workers=0, pin_memory=False,
    )

    model     = EEG2EEG(ch=CHANNELS).to(DEVICE)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=LR)
    steps_per_epoch = len(loader)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer, max_lr=LR, epochs=EPOCHS, steps_per_epoch=steps_per_epoch,
        pct_start=0.3, anneal_strategy="cos",
    )
    criterion = nn.MSELoss()

    model.train()
    for epoch in range(EPOCHS):
        total_loss = 0.0
        for xb, yb in loader:
            xb, yb = xb.to(DEVICE), yb.to(DEVICE)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            loss = criterion(model(xb), yb)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
            scheduler.step()
            total_loss += loss.item() * xb.size(0)
        print(f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{EPOCHS}  loss={total_loss/len(X_train):.6f}")

    # Inference
    model.eval()
    with torch.no_grad():
        pred_norm = model(torch.from_numpy(X_test).to(DEVICE)).cpu().numpy()

    # Denormalise to Sub03 original scale
    pred = pred_norm * std3 + mean3

    np.save(OUTPUT_PATH, pred)
    print(f"Saved -> {OUTPUT_PATH}  shape={pred.shape}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

r3_reasoning.content:
# Reasoning

## Approach

Round 2 timed out at exactly 180 s (exit code -9). The root cause was too many gradient steps: 30 epochs × ceil(16540/64) ≈ 7,800 steps on CPU. Round 3 targets ~260 steps total — comfortably under 180 s on CPU — while preserving the full EEG2EEG U-Net architecture.

Changes from round 2:
- **Epochs: 30 → 8** (primary lever).
- **Batch size: 64 → 512** — each epoch now has ceil(16540/512) ≈ 33 steps instead of 259, a ~8× reduction in compute per epoch.
- **OneCycleLR scheduler** — warm-up then cosine annealing, enabling good convergence in fewer epochs.
- Everything else (architecture, normalisation, denormalisation) is identical to round 2, which was logically correct.

The architecture is unchanged: 8-layer channel-wise 1-D U-Net (4 encoder blocks with MaxPool + 4 decoder blocks with linear upsample and skip connections), final Conv1d(kernel=1) to match output shape.

## Library choices

- **PyTorch** — specified in the available packages list; native GPU/CPU support, minimal boilerplate for custom U-Nets.
- **NumPy** — data loading and post-processing.
- No extra dependencies beyond the standard benchmark stack.

## Risks

1. **Underfitting** — 8 epochs with batch 512 is aggressive. OneCycleLR mitigates this by reaching a high learning rate quickly. If a 4th round were available, the fix would be to add epochs, not reduce batch.
2. **Data layout ambiguity** — the `to_images_first` guard (same as round 2) handles both `(17, N, 200)` and `(N, 17, 200)` on-disk layouts.
3. **Denormalisation** — predictions are produced in Sub03 normalised space and scaled back with Sub03 per-channel mean/std from the training set, consistent with the task description.

## Use of memory

Round 2 candidate.py provided the correct architecture and normalisation logic; only the training schedule caused the timeout. The exec.log confirmed a 180 s hard kill with no output produced. No eval.log existed (inference never ran). The fix is purely to the training schedule — the model definition, data paths, and output path are carried over unchanged.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: U-Net 8-layer channel-wise Conv1d in PyTorch, assumed disk layout (N,17,200), normalised with axis=(0,2), 30 epochs, batch 64, Adam lr=1e-3
rounds_summary.observation: Immediate crash: ValueError broadcast mismatch (17,200,200) vs (1,16540,1) — disk layout is (17,N,200) not (N,17,200), so mean had wrong shape

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Same U-Net with to_images_first() transpose guard fixing layout to (N,17,200), normalisation corrected, 30 epochs batch 64 Adam lr=1e-3
rounds_summary.observation: Hard timeout kill at 180s (exit_code=-9); 30 epochs x 259 steps/epoch on CPU far exceeds budget; no output produced

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Same U-Net architecture, epochs reduced 30->8, batch 512, OneCycleLR scheduler; ~260 total gradient steps estimated to fit 180s
rounds_summary.observation: Hard timeout kill at 180s again (exit_code=-9); even 8 epochs x 33 steps/epoch on CPU exceeds wall-clock budget

ruled_out_routes: PyTorch U-Net channel-wise Conv1d with batch_size=64, epochs=30, Adam lr=1e-3 on CPU — ~7800 steps, far exceeds 180s wall-clock
PyTorch U-Net channel-wise Conv1d with batch_size=512, epochs=8, OneCycleLR on CPU — ~260 steps still exceeds 180s wall-clock
Any PyTorch training loop over 16540 training samples on CPU using the full U-Net architecture without GPU — per-step cost alone defeats the 180s limit
content.synthesised_diagnosis: After R1's layout bug was fixed in R2, all subsequent failures are a single mechanism: 16540 samples x (17 channels x 200 timepoints) float32 tensors fed through an 8-layer Conv1d U-Net on CPU cannot complete even a minimal training loop within the 180s hard limit. Reducing epochs and increasing batch size (R3) cuts step count but not per-step wall time sufficiently; the bottleneck is CPU-bound tensor arithmetic, not iteration count.
content.rationale: The 180s CPU wall-clock limit is incompatible with any multi-epoch gradient-based training on the full 16540-sample dataset using PyTorch Conv1d ops; this is a resource-regime constraint, not a model logic error.
content.recommended_alternative: Replace the iterative training loop with a closed-form linear mapping: use numpy.linalg.lstsq to fit a per-channel linear regression from flattened Sub01 train vectors (16540 x 200) to Sub03 train vectors, then apply to test set. This runs in seconds on CPU via LAPACK, requires no epochs or batches, and produces a valid (200,17,200) prediction array. Specifically: for ch in range(17): coef,_,_,_ = np.linalg.lstsq(X_train[:,ch,:], Y_train[:,ch,:], rcond=None); pred[:,ch,:] = X_test[:,ch,:] @ coef.
